title: caltha palustris var. himalensis
intro: Caltha palustris var. himalensis, also known as Himalayan marsh marigold, is a variant of the marsh marigold plant, native to the Himalayan region. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are said to possess antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Caltha palustris var. himalensis include its potential to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its use in traditional medicine to soothe digestive problems and alleviate pain. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions for its medicinal uses, and it is also sometimes used in traditional remedies in the form of tinctures or ointments.
